title: Yogurt Flatbreads
description: Two-ingredient dough — just yogurt and flour — that comes together in minutes and cooks in a dry pan. Soft, slightly chewy, and ready to eat warm straight off the hob.
steps: Combine the flour, baking powder and salt in a bowl. Add the yogurt and mix with a fork, then knead briefly with your hands until you have a soft, slightly sticky dough. Do not overwork it.
Divide the dough into 6 equal balls. On a lightly floured surface, roll each one out thinly to about 3-4 mm thick.
Heat a dry cast-iron or non-stick pan over high heat until very hot. Cook each flatbread for 1-2 minutes per side until dark spots appear and the bread puffs slightly. No oil needed.
Stack the cooked flatbreads under a clean cloth to keep them soft, and serve warm.
footnote: Full-fat yogurt gives the best texture — low-fat versions make the dough too wet and the flatbreads less tender. The dough can be made a few hours ahead and kept covered in the fridge; bring it back to room temperature before rolling.
